name: Annie's Paramount Steak House

notes: The current restaurant-issued May 2026 dinner, lunch, and brunch PDFs plus the Spring 2025 happy-hour food list reconcile to 112 canonical products across 16 categories.

All 121 frozen rows were reconciled: 78 exact matches, three normalized matches, fourteen variant matches, seventeen stale products, and nine section/title artifacts. The 95 matched frozen rows cover 83 unique current products; 29 current products were omitted.

Repeated meal-period presentations are canonicalized to one product while retaining every applicable PDF source. Nine current happy-hour food products are retained; alcohol-oriented drink products are outside the food catalog.

The food PDFs publish product names and selected ingredient descriptions but no item-level allergen matrix. Eighty-eight current products have positive signals supported by explicit product or ingredient terms; twenty-four remain accurately unavailable.

Fifty-nine matched frozen allergen outcomes differ from the corrected evidence. The repair adds omitted direct positives and removes shifted or optional-protein signals that do not belong to the base product.
The generic consumer advisory to alert a server is not item-level contains, may-contain, shared-equipment, or negative evidence. No such claim is invented.
Optional shrimp and salmon add-ons are not smeared onto base salads or pasta, and coconut is not classified as a major tree nut.

The frozen output mixed an older Squarespace HTML catalog with multi-column dated PDFs, promoted nine headings/title cards into products, shifted descriptions and categories, and retained seventeen products absent from the current PDFs.

The reviewed catalog transcribes each dated PDF section independently, canonicalizes only repeated product presentations, retains per-period source URLs, and asserts product, category, and period counts.

Focused tests cover the 112-product catalog, every frozen-row disposition, optional-protein and coconut semantics, published repair invariants, and false shellfish regressions. The target-only repair is idempotent and the non-target restaurant projection is unchanged.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Confirmed Annie's Paramount Steak House at 1609 17th Street NW, Washington, DC 20009.

HTTP 200. Confirmed current menu navigation and links to dated dinner, lunch, brunch, happy-hour, and drink PDFs; embedded HTML rows are an older catalog and were not treated as current where they conflict.
HTTP 200. Visually inspected both restaurant-issued May 2026 dinner pages and reconciled 81 canonical products represented there.
HTTP 200. Visually inspected both restaurant-issued May 2026 lunch pages and reconciled 92 canonical products represented there.
HTTP 200. Visually inspected both restaurant-issued May 2026 brunch pages and reconciled 90 canonical products represented there.
HTTP 200. Visually inspected the restaurant-issued Spring 2025 happy-hour page and retained all nine food products while excluding five alcohol presentations.
HTTP 200. Visually inspected both pages; excluded thirty alcohol products and the separate non-food Heineken Zero product from the food catalog.
HTTP 404. The frozen configured singular /menu URL is no longer a usable source.
Targeted review of the official menu surface and all linked current documents found no item-level allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure beyond the generic consumer advisory.

findings.summary: The frozen extraction mixed an older HTML menu with multi-column PDFs, promoted nine headings/title cards into products, and shifted descriptions and categories across 84 matched rows.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt the catalog from visually reviewed, section-scoped dated PDF transcriptions and added structural count assertions.

findings.summary: Seventeen older products are absent from the dated menus, while 29 current products—including all current side products and happy-hour snacks—were missing from the app.
findings.resolution: Replaced the frozen catalog with all 112 current canonical food products and retained every applicable meal-period source.

findings.summary: Fifty-nine matched frozen rows had missing or overstated signals relative to explicit product and ingredient text in the current PDFs.
findings.resolution: Applied positive signals only from explicit product/ingredient terms and left 24 insufficiently disclosed products accurately unavailable.

findings.summary: Basil-Pine Nut Pesto Pasta, Country Chicken Salad, Feta Bacon Omelet, and Grilled Atlantic Salmon carried false shellfish signals from optional proteins or neighboring multi-column text.
findings.resolution: Made source rows section-scoped, excluded optional add-ons from base-item contains signals, and added focused false-shellfish regression assertions.

findings.summary: The PDFs publish only a generic instruction to alert the server; it cannot support item-level contains, may-contain, shared-equipment, or negative claims.
findings.resolution: Preserved the advisory as source context only and invented no item-level cross-contact or negative assurance.

findings.summary: The linked drink and happy-hour files contain 35 alcohol presentations plus one separate non-food zero-alcohol beer product.
findings.resolution: Kept all nine happy-hour foods and excluded the separate drink presentations from the food catalog.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Annie's current food catalog and corrected mixed-source structure, stale/omitted products, row associations, and allergen-source semantics.
